metadata.paper_title: SAAM: a method for analyzing the properties of software architectures
metadata.venue: ICSE '94: Proceedings of the 16th international conference on Software engineering

system_context.system_description: Architectures for developing the user interface portion of interactive systems.

evaluation_setup.evaluation_goal: To establish a method for describing and analyzing software architectures (SAAM) and demonstrate it by analyzing user interface architectures with respect to the quality of modifiability.

architectures.name: Serpent
architectures.description: Identifies a dialogue controller, the presentation and the application as three distinct processes. All communication is mediated by constraints on shared data in an active database.

key_components.responsibilities: Mediates the user's interaction with an application through the control of the presentation. Contains Dialogue Manager and Active Database.
Provide techniques for supporting interaction at both the logical and physical level.
Contain the computational semantics required for the application.

assumptions: There is typically only one application per run-time instance.
There is typically only one presentation module per run-time instance.

architectures.name: Chiron
architectures.description: Consists of a client and a server. The client consists of an application which exports abstract data types (ADTs). The server consists of an ADL, a virtual window system, and an instruction/event interpreter.

key_components.responsibilities: Contains ADL, Virtual Window System, and I/E Interpreter.
Maintains abstract representations of associated ADTs.

architectures.name: Garnet
architectures.description: A single process with Common Lisp and the X11 window system as its foundations. The Garnet toolkit is built on top of this foundation.

key_components.responsibilities: Fulfill functions of dialogue, functional core adapter, and functional core.

scenarios.scenario_text: Change the toolkit (e.g. a move from using Motif to Fresco).
Add a single option to a menu, reflecting some piece of application functionality which must be made available to a user.

scenario_classification.rationale: Classified as a 'modification' (Adaptation to new operating environments) requiring changes to the system.
Classified as a 'modification' (Extension of capabilities) involving adding new features.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Change Serpent's glue code; modifications to dialogue manager if new interaction capability is needed.
issues_identified: Serpent provides no architectural support; glue code is not isolated structurally.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Change in the behavior of the dialogue.
issues_identified: Serpent provides adequate architectural support; change is isolated in the dialogue manager.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Different toolkits could be inserted into the architecture as long as they comply with interface conventions.
issues_identified: Chiron provides significant support; strict separation of concerns aids modifiability.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Modification restricted to an Artist which is associated to the ADT containing the desired function.
Toolkit isolated to these three components which hide X11 dependencies.
Locating and modifying the affected Lisp code.
issues_identified: Not architecturally supported; dialogue is monolithic.

architectural_strengths: Chiron provides strict separation of concerns via the Virtual Window System.
Serpent isolates dialogue behavior in the Dialogue Manager.
Garnet isolates toolkit dependencies behind Interactors and Opal Graphics.
architectural_weaknesses: Serpent does not isolate logical interaction code (glue) structurally.
Garnet has monolithic dialogue, making menu changes difficult.
overall_results.modifiability_assessment: Systems with a clear allocation of the Arch/Slinky functional partitioning to their structure (like Chiron) provided better architectural support for the modifications.

architecture_comparison.rationale: Provides architectural support for both benchmark modifications (changing toolkit and adding menu option).
Provides support for adding a menu option but not for changing the toolkit.
Provides support for changing the toolkit but not for adding a menu option.

inference_notes: Inferred 'Indirect' classification for scenarios as they are explicitly described as 'classes of modifications' and 'benchmark tasks' to test modifiability.
Inferred ranking based on the number of scenarios supported (Chiron: 2/2, Serpent: 1/2, Garnet: 1/2).
